insights.headline: Japan AI Governance Mark: Gate to $180K Enterprise Market
insights.analysis: Japan's AI Governance Mark certification (launching 2026) is required for government procurement and creates instant credibility with Japanese enterprises. With Japan's innovation-first AI Promotion Act now enforced and 60% of companies deploying GenAI, NotVerify must pursue JIPDEC certification immediately to unlock access to 180K+ enterprises and government Gennai project partnerships. The regulatory requirement for explainability (XAI) perfectly validates NotVerify's Glass Box architecture as a compliance solution.

insights.headline: EasyTranslate Roll-Up Threatens Japan LSP Acquisition Window
insights.analysis: European competitor EasyTranslate is executing aggressive M&A strategy—acquiring traditional translation providers with deep client relationships and migrating them to centralized AI platforms. Combined with OpenAI's ChatGPT Translate launch (Jan 2026), the translation market is under siege from both consolidators and tech giants. NotVerify must accelerate Japanese LSP acquisitions before EasyTranslate enters Japan and position as the essential QA layer that sits on top of any translation engine (including ChatGPT).

insights.headline: 95% of Enterprises Shifting to Multi-Provider Translation Strategy
insights.analysis: 47.4% of enterprises now use multiple AI translation providers, 65.8% demand TMS integration, and 75% plan to deploy AI agents by end of 2026. This represents massive market churn and validates NotVerify's positioning as translation QA middleware. Enterprises don't trust single providers—they need orchestration, trust scoring, and compliance across multiple engines. Perfect timing to capture market share as legacy translation platforms lose grip.
